surface.name: Create an SAP BTP trial account and reach its Service Marketplace
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Create an SAP BTP trial account and reach its Service Marketplace. The official SAP tutorial provides a complete trial route, including registration, email activation, regional provisioning, cockpit navigation, and a marketplace terminal state. Prior surface before policy commit: Unresolved at global-account model, runtime environment, service, and first-workload selection.

alternatives_considered.name: SAP BTP Trial account and cockpit tour
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It terminates at account, subaccount, space, and Service Marketplace navigation rather than an application or service result, and its Cloud Foundry trial description must be reconciled with current Help Portal trial scope.

alternatives_considered.name: Cloud Foundry sample application with SAP Cloud SDK
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It assumes Cloud Foundry, Java, Maven, Cloud Foundry CLI, SAP Cloud SDK, and a deployment target.

alternatives_considered.name: Getting Started in the ABAP Environment
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer runtime with account, entitlement, system provisioning, Eclipse/ADT, and ABAP-specific work.

alternatives_considered.name: Kyma trial
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a Kubernetes-based runtime with cluster provisioning, kubectl, namespace, and workload-specific steps.

alternatives_considered.name: SAP Business Application Studio trial setup
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It reaches an IDE dev space, not a universal BTP workload result.

entry_point.developer_intent: Choose an SAP BTP account model, runtime, service, and observable first workload or platform result.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has not supplied an SAP account, company global account, contract, trial eligibility, runtime, service, region, language, or workload.
The developer may be an individual evaluator, existing SAP customer or partner, or member of an enterprise BTP account.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The SAP BTP trial getting-started tutorial ends when the cockpit's Service Marketplace shows its service tiles and filter bar.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Service tiles and the marketplace filter bar are visible.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Service tiles and the marketplace filter bar are visible.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Service tiles and the marketplace filter bar are visible.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: A declared use case, first-success outcome, and target SAP BTP runtime or service.
An SAP account plus access to a trial or enterprise SAP BTP global account.
For enterprise use, a customer, partner, consumption-based, subscription-based, or self-registered free-tier account path.
The relevant global-account, subaccount, entitlement, security, or runtime administrator roles for the selected workflow.

details: The page points to administrator and developer onboarding guides and a set of workflow families.
primary_path.input: Intent to start with SAP Business Technology Platform.

details: A trial account is for personal, non-productive exploration and has a limited lifecycle.
An enterprise global account can use paid and free-tier service plans and is tied to a contract or self-registration route.
Existing company accounts require coordination with their global-account administrator.
primary_path.input: Individual evaluation versus organizational development or production needs.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected model matches intended use, ownership, lifecycle, and commercial requirements.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The roster supplies no account or contract context.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to an SAP account and obtain access to the selected BTP global account.
details: The trial tutorial uses sap.com registration, email activation, BTP Trial Try Now, and cockpit access.
Enterprise paths may require purchase, partner participation, self-registration, invitation, or administrator coordination.
primary_path.input: SAP identity, eligible organization or trial route, and any required contract or invitation.
primary_path.success_signal: The SAP BTP cockpit opens at the global account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Registration, email activation, account approval, contract, invitation, or company administrator steps vary by account model.

primary_path.action: Choose Cloud Foundry, ABAP, Kyma, or another service-specific workflow.
details: The official Getting Started page lists Cloud Foundry, ABAP, and Kyma as separate environment workflows.
The BTP catalog also requires choosing services and plans by use case, entitlement, provider, and region.
primary_path.input: Application architecture, language, SAP integration needs, runtime preference, and required service.
primary_path.output: One runtime or service onboarding guide.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected guide matches the workload and is available in the account and region.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Runtime and service availability, quotas, and trial scope can constrain the choice.

primary_path.action: Choose the concrete first workload and follow its prerequisites, provisioning, tools, deployment, and verification.
details: A Cloud Foundry sample can require an org, space, Cloud Foundry CLI, Java, Maven, and application push.
Kyma requires cluster and Kubernetes tooling.
ABAP requires environment and development-system provisioning and ABAP tooling.
Business Application Studio requires a subscription and dev space.
primary_path.input: Chosen runtime, service, language, region, and terminal success.
primary_path.output: An atomic first-mile path with a defined completion signal.
primary_path.success_signal: A guide-specific resource or workload result can be observed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Research stops rather than asserting that one of these materially different results represents SAP BTP as a whole.

primary_path.action: Open sap.com and select Log On.
details: The tutorial starts from the SAP website before BTP provisioning.
primary_path.input: A browser and an email identity.
primary_path.output: The SAP sign-in or registration experience.
primary_path.success_signal: The user can either sign in or choose registration.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: None documented for this navigation step.

primary_path.action: If no SAP account exists, select Register, complete the required fields, and submit the form.
details: An existing SAP account can bypass registration and continue with sign-in.
primary_path.input: The required account identity and profile fields.
primary_path.success_signal: SAP reports that an activation email was sent.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid or incomplete required fields block submission.

primary_path.action: Deliver the SAP account activation email.
details: The developer must wait for this message before activating a newly registered account.
primary_path.output: An email containing the activation link.
primary_path.success_signal: The activation email arrives in the inbox.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Email delivery is an explicit wait; without the message, a new account cannot proceed.

primary_path.action: Open the activation link and complete account activation.
details: The tutorial expects a successful activation confirmation.
primary_path.success_signal: A success message confirms that the account is activated.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An invalid or expired activation link requires account-recovery or registration support outside the tutorial.

primary_path.action: Open SAP BTP Trial, select Try Now, and follow the sign-in instructions.
details: This enters the BTP trial onboarding flow using the activated SAP account.
primary_path.output: The SAP BTP trial setup flow.
primary_path.success_signal: The BTP trial region and account-creation controls are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Authentication failure or trial ineligibility prevents provisioning.

primary_path.action: Choose the trial geographic region and select Create Account.
details: The trial has region and resource constraints, so this choice determines the provisioned location.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A region may be constrained or unavailable under the trial offering.

primary_path.action: Provision the SAP BTP trial account and its initial resources.
details: The tutorial shows a setup dialog and instructs the developer to wait.
primary_path.input: The submitted region and account request.
primary_path.success_signal: The setup dialog reports completion and enables Continue.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Provisioning is an explicit wait; setup errors must be resolved before the cockpit is available.

primary_path.action: Select Continue to open the SAP BTP cockpit.
details: The cockpit is the management surface for the newly provisioned trial.
primary_path.output: The BTP cockpit trial account view.
primary_path.success_signal: The cockpit loads and shows the trial account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A still-running setup leaves Continue unavailable.

primary_path.action: Open the trial subaccount tile and inspect its status, Cloud Foundry API endpoint, and space.
details: These values demonstrate that the trial subaccount and Cloud Foundry environment were provisioned.
primary_path.input: The BTP cockpit trial account view.
primary_path.success_signal: The subaccount view exposes its state plus the Cloud Foundry API and space information.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing or incomplete environment details indicate provisioning has not finished successfully.

primary_path.action: Open Services, then Service Marketplace, and verify that the service catalog and filter control are visible.
details: The official tutorial ends its orientation by showing the services available to the trial subaccount.
primary_path.success_signal: Service tiles and the marketplace filter bar are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the marketplace does not load, the documented trial-account terminal has not been reached.

branches.condition: The developer selects the individual SAP BTP Trial tutorial.
branches.path: Open sap.com, click Log On, choose Register if needed, fill the required registration fields, submit, wait for the activation email, click its activation control, observe activation success, open the SAP BTP Trial page, select Try Now, follow signup instructions, open the trial cockpit, choose a geographic region, click Create Account, wait for provisioning, click Continue, open the trial subaccount tile, inspect its state and runtime details, open Services, select Service Marketplace, and verify services and plan filters are visible.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Defines success as trial cockpit, subaccount, space, and marketplace orientation, not a deployed workload.

branches.condition: Automatic Cloud Foundry trial setup fails and Cloud Foundry remains available for the selected account.
branches.path: Enter the trial global account, choose New Subaccount, set display name, provider, region, and subdomain, create it, open its tile, choose Enable Cloud Foundry, open Entitlements, configure entitlements, add service plans, maximize numerical quotas, save, open Cloud Foundry Spaces, create a dev space, and verify the trial setup is ready.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds manual subaccount, org, entitlement, quota, and space administration before development.

branches.condition: The selected outcome is a first Java application on Cloud Foundry.
branches.path: Obtain a BTP Cloud Foundry account, install and authenticate the Cloud Foundry CLI, install Java and Maven, generate the SAP Cloud SDK sample project, build it, configure its manifest and target, push it to the selected org and space, wait for staging and start, obtain the route, and open the application to verify Hello World.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Defines success as a reachable deployed Java application and adds local tooling, code generation, cloud staging, and route work.

branches.condition: The selected runtime is Kyma trial.
branches.path: Create or access the trial account, create the single permitted Kyma environment, wait for cluster provisioning, obtain Kubernetes access, choose namespace and workload, deploy it, wait for Kubernetes readiness, expose it if needed, and verify the workload or endpoint.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds cluster provisioning and Kubernetes resource steps; the current trial page states the Kyma runtime expires after 14 days.

branches.condition: The selected first result is SAP Business Application Studio access.
branches.path: Sign up for a BTP trial, open the trial cockpit, use Quick Tool Access to open SAP Business Application Studio, click Create Dev Space, choose its type and extensions, wait for creation, start it, and open the dev space.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Defines success as an available cloud IDE rather than a deployed app.

branches.condition: The developer uses an enterprise account with free-tier service plans.
branches.path: Self-register for or obtain access to an enterprise global account, select or create the correct global account, coordinate with its administrator, create or select a subaccount, assign entitlements and free-tier plans, configure identity and roles, then enable the chosen runtime or service.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds organizational ownership, admin, entitlement, region, quota, and possible commercial coordination while enabling longer-lived work.

friction_gates.description: Trial versus enterprise/free-tier account changes eligibility, lifecycle, support, entitlements, and allowed use.
Trial is limited to personal, non-productive exploration and is not permitted for production or team development.
An SAP identity and access to a BTP global account are required.
New sap.com registration includes email activation.
Enterprise access may require contract, purchase, partner, invitation, or global-account administrator coordination.
Cloud Foundry, ABAP, Kyma, Business Application Studio, and service-specific paths are peer options.
Runtime enablement, entitlement allocation, role collection, and subaccount administration require appropriate roles.
Provider, region, subdomain, entitlements, quotas, org, space, and service plans can be required before a workload.
Tooling depends on the route and may include Cloud Foundry CLI, kubectl, Java, Maven, Eclipse/ADT, or cloud IDE setup.
Account, subaccount, org, cluster, ABAP system, dev space, service, and application provisioning can add undocumented waits.

sources.title: SAP BTP Getting Started
evidence_supported: Global account, Cloud Foundry, ABAP, and Kyma workflow families

sources.title: Getting a Global Account
sections_used: Try Out SAP BTP for Free
Sign up for a Customer Account

sources.title: Get an Account on SAP BTP Trial
sections_used: Log on to SAP BTP Trial
evidence_supported: Full candidate trial signup and activation path
Cockpit, subaccount, space, and marketplace terminal state

sources.title: Trial Accounts and Free Tier
sections_used: Trial Account or Free Tier Offering

sources.title: Setting Up Your Trial Account
sections_used: Create Your Trial Org and Configure Entitlements
evidence_supported: Org, entitlement, quota, and space actions

sources.title: Getting Started with a Trial Account in SAP Business Application Studio
sections_used: Getting Started with a Trial Account
evidence_supported: Trial, cockpit, Quick Tool Access, and Create Dev Space sequence

sources.title: Create a Sample Application on Cloud Foundry Using SAP Cloud SDK
evidence_supported: Java, Maven, Cloud Foundry CLI, build, staging, route, and app verification

uncertainties.question: Which account model, runtime, and service should define SAP BTP first success?
uncertainties.impact: It changes organizational approval, entitlement, roles, tools, provisioning, and terminal result.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official Getting Started page explicitly delegates this choice to the use case.

uncertainties.question: Should the platform boundary be account readiness, a provisioned runtime, a service instance, an IDE dev space, or a deployed workload?
uncertainties.impact: These boundaries differ by dozens of actions and multiple administrator/developer handoffs.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: SAP provides all of these as getting-started outcomes and does not name one universal milestone.

uncertainties.question: Is Cloud Foundry still available in every new individual trial described by the 2025 tutorial?
uncertainties.impact: The detailed trial account and Cloud Foundry candidate may not be executable for a new 2026 trial user.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The current About the Trial Account page describes Kyma trial scope, while the maintained 2025 trial tutorial and current Cloud Foundry setup page still describe a trial subaccount, org, and space.

uncertainties.question: What current live registration and enterprise-contract screens occur before cockpit access?
uncertainties.impact: The common account gate cannot be counted completely for every persona.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: SAP docs describe account paths and email activation but enterprise purchase, invitation, and company administration vary.

excluded_after_success: No runtime-specific downstream work can be excluded until account model, environment, service, first workload, and terminal success are selected.
